bugs_found_by_this_one_dollar: CHECKOUT LOOP: PaywallModal pre-flighted the session with the BROWSER supabase client, which cannot read httpOnly cookies. Every signed-in user was bounced back to /login forever. NO CUSTOMER COULD EVER HAVE PAID.
ADMIN DESTRUCTION: all 3 webhook handlers wrote the role blindly. Paying would have replaced role=admin with 'subscriber'; cancelling would have set role='free', stripping admin entirely.
UNPROVABLE PAID PATH: entitlement keyed only on role='subscriber', so a paying admin could never register as a customer.
bug_i_introduced_and_had_to_own: My webhook-secret probe stripped the whsec_ prefix before computing the HMAC. Stripe keys the HMAC with the FULL secret. The probe therefore returned 400 for ANY secret and reported SECRET DOES NOT MATCH twice — falsely. Michael rotated production twice on the strength of a test that could not have passed. The secret was correct the entire time. A broken test that reports FAIL is not safer than one that reports PASS.

north_star.status: COMPUTABLE — first time in program history
